description: Cardio health focused dashboard: blood pressure, training load, readiness, aerobic/anaerobic training effect, lactate threshold, VO2 max, fitness age, and RHR trends. Panels with _normalRangeMetric markers get profile-driven population bands from USER_AGE/USER_SEX env vars.

panels.description: AI-generated wellness summary updated each fetch cycle. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in env.

options.defaultContent:
# AI Health Summary

*Waiting for first summary generation. Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and wait for a fetch cycle.*

targets.query: SELECT last("summary") AS "summary" FROM "AIHealthSummary" WHERE "dashboard" = 'cardio_health'

panels.title: AI Wellness Summary

targets.query: SELECT last("Systolic") FROM "BloodPressure" WHERE $timeFilter
SELECT last("Diastolic") FROM "BloodPressure" WHERE $timeFilter
SELECT last("score") FROM "TrainingReadiness" WHERE $timeFilter
SELECT last("VO2_max_value") FROM "VO2_Max" WHERE $timeFilter
SELECT last("fitnessAge") FROM "FitnessAge" WHERE $timeFilter
SELECT last("restingHeartRate") FROM "DailyStats" WHERE $timeFilter

panels.description: Systolic blood pressure trend. AHA bands: green <120 (Normal), yellow 120-139 (Elevated/Stage 1), red >=140 (Stage 2 HTN). Dashed line = your 14-day rolling typical.

targets.query: SELECT mean("Systolic") FROM "BloodPressure" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("Systolic"), 14) FROM "BloodPressure" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Systolic blood pressure

panels.description: Diastolic blood pressure trend. AHA bands: green <80 (Normal), yellow 80-89 (Stage 1 HTN), red >=90 (Stage 2 HTN). Dashed line = your 14-day rolling typical.

targets.query: SELECT mean("Diastolic") FROM "BloodPressure" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("Pulse") FROM "BloodPressure" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("Diastolic"), 14) FROM "BloodPressure" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Diastolic blood pressure & pulse

panels.description: Weekly cumulative training load. Trend direction matters: rising = building fitness, falling = detraining or tapering. Dashed line = your 14-day rolling typical.

targets.query: SELECT mean("weeklyTrainingLoad") FROM "TrainingStatus" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("weeklyTrainingLoad"), 14) FROM "TrainingStatus" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Weekly training load

panels.description: Acute:Chronic Workload Ratio. Sweet spot 0.8-1.3 (green) = progressive overload with low injury risk. >1.5 (red) = spike/injury risk. <0.6 (red) = detraining.

targets.query: SELECT mean("dailyAcuteChronicWorkloadRatio") FROM "TrainingStatus" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("dailyAcuteChronicWorkloadRatio"), 14) FROM "TrainingStatus" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Acute:Chronic workload ratio

panels.description: Daily acute (recent) vs chronic (rolling) training load. The gap between them drives ACWR — when acute spikes well above chronic, injury risk rises.

targets.query: SELECT mean("dailyTrainingLoadAcute") FROM "TrainingStatus" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("dailyTrainingLoadChronic") FROM "TrainingStatus" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Acute vs chronic training load

panels.description: Training Readiness score with the contributing factor percentages. HRV and sleep factors are the most actionable — if HRV factor is dragging score down, you're under-recovered.

targets.query: SELECT mean("score") FROM "TrainingReadiness" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("hrvFactorPercent") FROM "TrainingReadiness" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("sleepScoreFactorPercent") FROM "TrainingReadiness" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("stressHistoryFactorPercent") FROM "TrainingReadiness" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("score"), 14) FROM "TrainingReadiness" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Training readiness & contributing factors

panels.description: Aerobic (base-building / zone 2) vs anaerobic (intensity / intervals) training effect per workout. Scale: 0=none, 1-2=minor, 2-3=maintaining, 3-4=improving, 4-5=highly improving/overreaching.

targets.query: SELECT mean("aerobicTrainingEffect") FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("anaerobicTrainingEffect") FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Aerobic vs anaerobic training effect

panels.description: Per-workout training load (EPOC-based) and daily moderate + vigorous intensity minutes. Higher load = bigger training stimulus. Dashed line = your 14-day rolling typical load.

targets.query: SELECT mean("activityTrainingLoad") FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("activityTrainingLoad"), 14) FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Activity training load

panels.description: VO2 max trend — the gold-standard measure of cardio fitness. Only updates after GPS-tracked running or cycling. Dashed line = your 14-day rolling typical. Population band from Cooper Institute norms for your age/sex bracket.

targets.query: SELECT mean("VO2_max_value") FROM "VO2_Max" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("VO2_max_value_cycling") FROM "VO2_Max" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("VO2_max_value"), 14) FROM "VO2_Max" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: VO2 max trend

panels.description: Garmin Fitness Age vs your chronological age. Lower is better — the gap represents your cardio fitness relative to age-matched norms.

targets.query: SELECT mean("fitnessAge") FROM "FitnessAge" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("chronologicalAge") FROM "FitnessAge" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("achievableFitnessAge") FROM "FitnessAge" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("fitnessAge"), 14) FROM "FitnessAge" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Fitness age vs chronological age

panels.description: Long-term resting heart rate from DailyStats. A declining RHR over weeks/months is the clearest sign your cardio fitness is improving. Population band from Cooper Institute + AHA norms.

targets.query: SELECT mean("restingHeartRate") FROM "DailyStats" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("restingHeartRate"), 14) FROM "DailyStats" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Resting heart rate (long-term trend)

panels.description: Lactate threshold heart rate for running. A rising LTHR means your body can sustain higher intensities aerobically — direct cardio fitness improvement.

targets.query: SELECT mean("HeartRateThreshold_running") FROM "LactateThreshold" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("SpeedThreshold_running") FROM "LactateThreshold" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("HeartRateThreshold_running"), 14) FROM "LactateThreshold" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Lactate threshold HR & speed (running)

panels.description: Garmin Endurance Score — long-term aerobic capacity composite. Dashed line = your 14-day rolling typical.

targets.query: SELECT mean("EnduranceScore") FROM "EnduranceScore" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT moving_average(mean("EnduranceScore"), 14) FROM "EnduranceScore" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Endurance score

panels.description: Hill Score with strength vs endurance breakdown. Overall score shows climbing ability composite; the components tell you what to train.

targets.query: SELECT mean("overallScore") FROM "HillScore" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("strengthScore") FROM "HillScore" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)
SELECT mean("enduranceScore") FROM "HillScore" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Hill score (overall / strength / endurance)

title: Cardio Health
